response: I would tell my friend I can't look at the photos and neither should they. First I'd ask what's going on with their probation, then offer to study together tonight with our own notes and suggest they talk to an advisor about appeal or bursary options tomorrow.

It wouldn't change my decision. Even if others have the photos, using them is still cheating and the risk of expulsion is worse than failing. I'd still refuse and focus on the legitimate supports available to my friend.
That's tough. I would probably try to help somehow and maybe talk to someone about it if it kept happening, because it isn't fair to the others covering for them.
I guess whether it keeps happening and how the manager might react.
The patient asked me not to make a big deal of it, so honestly I would respect their wishes and stay out of it. It's between them and the other volunteer.
Nothing, since the patient asked me to keep it quiet and I don't want to cause drama at the clinic.
